top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): You handed me 8 single JPEGs and called them '5-bracket exposures per scene across 6 scenes' — that's 30 RAW bracket files I need and you delivered exactly 0. Without the actual exposure brackets there is no HDR merge, no load-bearing window-pull, and the entire grading workflow collapses on step one.

AGENT CHOKE on 'bracketed_frames[0]': document_merge_data_vector will never even get its turn — the agent will choke immediately trying to run image_select_by_prompt('windows') and image_adjust_highlights on a flat single-exposure JPEG passed off as a 5-bracket set. There is no luminosity range to blend, so the masked window-pull step has nothing to pull from and the pipeline hard-fails at the first HDR composite call.
Completeness 5/10: 24 of 30 required bracket frames are missing — image_apply_auto_tone and HDR blend have no exposure range to work from
Executability 5/10: image_adjust_exposure / HDR blend steps require multi-exposure input; single-frame delivery means the entire bracket-merge sub-chain cannot execute
qc_gaps: Per-asset self-QC averages 8.9/10, but package Executability scored 5 — assets are individually strong yet wrong/insufficient for the task.
